intro: Ophryosporus densiflorus, also known as the "Dense-flowered Ophryosporus" or "Tawa", is a plant species native to the Andean region, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from various health issues such as fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ophryosporus densiflorus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Herbal preparations made from this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory conditions, such as bronchitis and asthma.
